_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
The workspace operates as a freight forwarder. Across individual transactions, it may also act as customs broker, importer of record, or intermediary placing packaging on the EU market on behalf of clients. The workspace's compliance position in the EU regulatory chain varies by instrument and by transaction type:

**As freight forwarder:** The workspace is not the primary regulated entity under emissions regulations that place obligations on vessel operators, aircraft operators, or vehicle manufacturers. However, the workspace is downstream of carriers who bear those obligations, and cost pass-through from those obligations reaches the workspace's client pricing and margin.

**As customs broker or agent:** The workspace is within scope of the Union Customs Code obligations applicable to customs representatives. Any ICS2 (Import Control System 2) filing obligations applicable to air and maritime imports into the EU fall on the workspace when it acts as filing party. *Source: Regulation (EU) No 952/2013 — Union Customs Code, EUR-Lex / Official Journal, https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX%3A32013R0952.*

**As party commissioning or placing packaging on the EU market:** For any transaction in which the workspace commissions packaging fabrication, imports packaged goods into the EU as importer of record, or supplies packaging to EU clients, the workspace may be a "producer" under PPWR (Regulation (EU) 2025/40). Legal Confirmation Required: whether specific transaction types in the workspace's live events, fine art, or high-value automotive verticals constitute "placing packaging on the market" under PPWR Article 3 definitions.

**As transport service provider issuing emissions data to clients:** Once the EU transport emissions accounting regulation (COM(2023) 441) is adopted and in force, the workspace will be required to provide clients with harmonised GHG emissions data for transport services procured. This obligation falls directly on transport service providers. The political agreement was reached in November 2025. *Source: European Commission — DG MOVE, November 2025, https://transport.ec.europa.eu/news-events/news/commission-welcomes-political-agreement-new-rules-harmonise-transport-emissions-calculations-eu-2025-11-06_en.*

**Role placement note:** The workspace's exact compliance obligations under each instrument depend on its contractual role in each transaction. Legal Confirmation Required on role classification for each instrument where the workspace's position is not unambiguous from the regulation's definitions.

---
